name: woodworking-plan
description: Woodworking Plan — mobile-first PWA repository of woodworking plans. Sprint 0: environment & architecture.

scripts.// postinstall: Prisma's client is generated code, not shipped in node_modules. Without this, any fresh install (local, CI, or Vercel) builds against a client that does not exist yet. Running it on postinstall means it can never be forgotten.
scripts.// vercel-build: Runs on Vercel. check-db-urls FIRST, because in Sprint 6 Vercel's DIRECT_URL pointed at the DEV database while DATABASE_URL pointed at PRODUCTION: every deploy migrated dev, reported 'No pending migrations to apply' (true, of dev), and left production's schema frozen. Build green, deploy green, production 500ing on every plan page. A build that migrates one database and serves another must fail, not ship.
scripts.vercel-build: node scripts/check-db-urls.mjs && prisma generate && prisma migrate deploy && next build
scripts.// db scripts: Prisma's CLI only reads .env — but .env.local is the Next.js convention and the file that actually holds our secrets. dotenv-cli bridges the two so DATABASE_URL is visible to migrate/seed/studio. Without this, every db command fails with 'Environment variable not found: DATABASE_URL'. db:generate is excluded on purpose: it needs no database connection, and Vercel/CI run it with no .env.local present.
scripts.// images:migrate: One-off + re-runnable: re-hosts plan photos onto R2 and rewrites content/plans/*.json (DECISIONS_LOG 2026-07-17). Loads R2_* creds from .env.local via dotenv-cli. Idempotent; supports --dry-run and --force.
scripts.// images:null-unresolved: Run AFTER images:migrate. HEAD-verifies any remaining source-host image URLs and, for confirmed 404s, moves them to unresolvedImages (source URL preserved) and empties images so the plan shows the placeholder. Format-preserving, idempotent; supports --dry-run.

comment:overrides: postcss <8.5.10 has a moderate XSS advisory (CVE-2026-41305). Next.js still pins a vulnerable range, so npm reports 'no fix available'. Forcing the patched postcss resolves it. Remove this override once Next.js ships a version that depends on postcss >=8.5.10.
